---
title: "DeepSeek vs ChatGPT on Ethical Questions"
description: "ChatGPT seems to follow deontological ethics (rules-based) while DeepSeek aligns with consequentialism (outcome-based). Pre-training or RL-tuning?"
author: "synesis"
date: "2025-02-08"
categories: [AI, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, generative AI, ethics, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

!["I asked DeepSeek vs ChatGPT a series of ethical questions — and the results were shocking." Image: Tom's Guide. ([Source](https://www.tomsguide.com/ai/i-asked-deepseek-vs-chatgpt-a-series-of-ethical-questions-and-the-results-were-shocking))](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

The observation seems to be ChatGPT follows deontological ethics (rules-based), while DeepSeek aligns with consequentialism (outcome-based). It'd be interesting to learn whether this is the result of pre-training or RL-tuning. [[1]](#ref-1)

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] "[I asked DeepSeek vs ChatGPT a series of ethical questions — and the results were shocking](https://www.tomsguide.com/ai/i-asked-deepseek-vs-chatgpt-a-series-of-ethical-questions-and-the-results-were-shocking)." *Tom's Guide*, February 2025.

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_i-asked-deepseek-vs-chatgpt-a-series-of-ethical-activity-7294111865167589378-TTu7).*
